caption: The video discusses the suspension of Burkina Faso from all African Union (AU) activities, as announced by the African Union Political Affairs Peace and Security Council. The tweet shown in the video explains that this decision was made in accordance with relevant AU instruments, including the AU Constitutive Act, AUPSC Protocol, and the African Charter on Democracy, Elections & Governance. The suspension is effective until the restoration of constitutional order in Burkina Faso. This action comes one week after military members seized the government, indicating a response to a significant political upheaval in the country. The video aims to inform viewers about this political development through clear and concise English language content, as part of VOA60's Learning English series.
